commit.message:
docs: analyze existing iOS logging - NOT sufficient for diagnosis

Current logging reviewed:
- G7CGMManager logs 'activated at \(activatedAt)' but just Date, not epoch
- GlucoseStorage logs treatment but not raw JSON content
- NightscoutManager logs 'Treatments uploaded' but NOT the payload
- NightscoutTreatment JSON encoding has NO logging

Critical gaps identified:
1. No logging of actual JSON payload sent to Nightscout
2. No logging of created_at ISO8601 string after encoding
3. No comparison of Date object vs encoded string

Added enhanced logging proposal with specific code snippets for:
- NightscoutManager.uploadNonCoreDataTreatments() - log rawJSON
- GlucoseStorage.createCGMStateTreatment() - log epoch value
- Future date detection warning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add diagnostic investigation matrix for 2^32 overflow bug

Add comprehensive debugging strategy:
- Investigation matrix mapping each code location to verification method
- Three diagnostic tests with exact code snippets to add
- Data collection request for affected users
- Narrowed suspicion list with primary suspect: JSON encoding path

Key suspects identified:
1. NightscoutTreatment.rawJSON encoding (most likely)
2. G7CGMManagerState persistence round-trip
3. ISO8601DateFormatter edge case

Code paths traced:
- G7Sensor → G7CGMManager.state.activatedAt → PluginSource.sensorStartDate
- BloodGlucose.sessionStartDate → GlucoseStorage.createCGMStateTreatment
- NightscoutTreatment.createdAt → JSONCoding.encoder → upload

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: CRITICAL FINDING - 2^32 overflow pattern in future-dated entries

Mathematical analysis of the bug data proves:
- Difference between wrong (2161) and correct (2025) dates = EXACTLY 2^32 seconds
- The activationDate calculation is NOT the bug (notes field shows correct date)
- Bug is in serialization/upload chain where 2^32 is added to epoch

Key evidence:
  notes: '89N9W6 activated on 2025-06-04 19:56:03' (CORRECT)
  created_at: '2161-07-12T02:24:18.577Z' (WRONG = correct + 2^32 sec)

This shifts the investigation from BLE parsing to:
1. iOS Date <-> TimeInterval conversion
2. JSON encoding type confusion
3. Network layer byte manipulation
4. 32-bit to 64-bit integer extension

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add individual AFL startup commands to docs/afl and docs/iccDEV/afl

Add per-target copy-paste startup commands for all 9 AFL fuzzers with

commit.message:
docs: add cross-codebase G7 EGV parsing comparison

Compare G7 0x4E message parsing across 4 codebases:
- G7SensorKit (Loop/Trio)
- DiaBLE
- xDrip+ (Android)
- xDrip4iOS

Key findings:
- All agree on byte layout for most fields
- ⚠️ xDrip4iOS reads 'age' as 1 byte instead of 2 (potential bug)
- Date calculation approaches differ significantly
- xDrip4iOS uses simpler now-age approach, avoiding activation date issues
- G7SensorKit calculates activationDate which can go wrong

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Redesign checkout + fix free template download/share

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add message inventory logging strategy for firmware format drift

Hypothesis: Future-dated timestamps may indicate CGM firmware version changes
affecting byte field layouts. Propose comprehensive logging strategy:

- Log ALL BLE messages with raw hex for format inventory
- Add firmware version identification logging
- Create server-side message collection via devicestatus
- Reference xDrip+ FirmwareCapability.java firmware version registry
- Add 3 new investigation tasks for message inventory/logging

This enables detection of format drift across firmware versions and
collection of raw BLE samples for root cause analysis.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: update future-dated analysis with G7SensorKit missing fix

Found that CGMBLEKit PR #191 stopgap was applied to G6 path only.
G7SensorKit still creates PersistedCgmEvent without date validation.

Key findings:
- G6 (CGMBLEKit): TransmitterManager.swift:340-347 has filter
- G7 (G7SensorKit): G7CGMManager.swift:311 has NO filter
- Both Loop and Trio use same unfixed G7SensorKit code
- Server-side rejection could cause infinite retry loop

Added recommended fix strategy with phased approach:
1. Client-side validation in G7CGMManager
2. Server-side warning (then rejection)
3. Comprehensive message parsing audit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add GAP-API-021 for future-dated entries validation

Analyzes Loop#2087 and cgm-remote-monitor#8453 where future-dated
entries (e.g., year 2161) corrupt SAGE/CAGE pills.

Root cause: No MAX_TIMESTAMP validation in API v3, combined with
client-side bugs where negative minutesSinceStart values produce
future dates via Date() - (-negative).

Added:
- GAP-API-021: No Rejection of Future-Dated Entries
- REQ-TS-001: Reject entries >24h in future
- REQ-TS-002: Document existing MIN_TIMESTAMP validation
- future-dated-entries-analysis.md backlog investigation doc

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

analysis: onboard 2 OOM artifacts with distinct code paths

- oom-CIccSampledCurveSegment-SetSize-IccTagLut_cpp-Line4121.icc
  Entry: CIccTagLutAtoB::Read → CIccSegmentedCurve → SetSize (CWE-400)
  spac v5, 3133B — different call chain than existing 3 OOM artifacts

- oom-CIccSingleSampledCurve-SetSize-IccMpeBasic_cpp-Line1501.icc
  Entry: CIccMpeCurveSet::Read → CIccSingleSampledCurve::SetSize (CWE-400)
  mntr v255, 5480B — different class (SingleSampled vs Segmented)

2 redundant variants (crash-e1e7, crash-fb96) discarded — same site as above.
Upstream tools reject gracefully (exit 0/255, 0 ASAN/UBSAN).
iccanalyzer-lite: 0 ASAN errors on both.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace DevAuthService with real ServerAuthService for WebApp

- Created ServerAuthService: uses Identity's UserManager directly for
  register/login in server-side Blazor context
- Auto-sign-in endpoint (/account-action/AutoSignIn): generates a
  one-time token since Blazor Server can't set cookies over WebSocket
- RegisterPage.razor: detects userId|token in result and redirects
  to HTTP endpoint for cookie sign-in, then to /onboarding
- LoginPage.razor: same auto-sign-in redirect pattern

E2E verified: Register → onboarding, Login → Dashboard both work.

* Add navigation test harness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement OCCT solid navigation mapping

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SurfaceNormal location mismatch and CreatePolyhedron deflection scaling (#6)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct SurfaceNormal location mixing and use relative meshing deflection

- SurfaceNormal: obtain surface + location via BRep_Tool::Surface(face, loc),
  transform query point into surface local frame before GeomAPI_ProjectPointOnSurf
  so the (u,v) parameters match the BRepAdaptor_Surface domain used by
  TryGetOutwardNormal; delegate final normal computation to that helper.
  Remove the now-unused kMinNormalMagnitudeSquared constant.

- CreatePolyhedron: replace hard-coded kLinearDeflection = 0.1 with
  kRelativeDeflection = 0.01 and isRelative=true so mesh density scales
  with each face's bounding-box size rather than being a fixed world length.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: build complete Express.js + TypeScript backend for Surgery Planner

- server/package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts: project config
- src/index.ts + src/app.ts: Express app with helmet, CORS, rate limiting
- src/config/: typed env config, singleton Prisma client
- src/middleware/: JWT auth, Zod validate, error handler, notFound, CSRF protection
- src/modules/auth/: login (bcrypt+JWT), httpOnly refresh cookie, logout
- src/modules/templates/: full CRUD with filters, pagination, search
- src/modules/blueprints/: CRUD + status PATCH, surgeon-scoped access control
- src/modules/users/: admin user mgmt + /me profile with password change
- src/modules/audit/: admin-only audit log listing with pagination
- prisma/schema.prisma: SQLite schema (User, SurgeryTemplate, SurgeryBlueprint, AuditLog)

commit.message:
ci: add 31 JSON -cfg tests to iccDEV tool workflow (103→134)

Add .github/scripts/iccdev-json-cfg-tests.sh — CI-compatible JSON
configuration test suite covering iccApplyNamedCmm, iccApplySearch,
and iccApplyProfiles `-cfg` mode.

31 tests across 7 sections:
- Valid configs (7): basic, 8bit, 16bit, output-file, debug+BPC, chain, booleans
- Search (1): ApplySearch with valid profile
- Encoding variations (5): float, unitFloat, percent, 16BitV2, value
- Intent variations (4): perceptual, relative, saturation, absolute
- Malformed JSON (9): empty, array, syntax, null, missing fields, wrong types,
  extreme values, nonexistent profiles, path traversal
- Cross-tool (5): malformed configs for ApplySearch + ApplyProfiles

Dynamically generates JSON configs using discovered ICC profiles in CI.
ASAN/UBSAN detection with separate exit codes (0=pass, 1=fail, 2=sanitizer).

Local verification: 31/31 pass, 0 ASAN, 0 UBSAN.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
cfl: add CFL-026 JSON toJson() key typo fixes (5 sites)

CFL-026 fixes 5 fromJson/toJson round-trip bugs in IccCmmConfig.cpp:
- Line 303: dstDigits] → dstDigits (stray bracket in key string)
- Line 422,424: srcImgFile/dstImgFile → srcImageFile/dstImageFile
- Line 681: iccProfile → iccFile (match fromJson key)
- Line 705,1089: icInterpNames → icInterpNames[i] (array vs element)

All affect toJson() serialization — fromJson() parsing is correct.
Verified: 12/12 fuzzers build, 90/90 JSON tests pass, 0 ASAN/UBSAN.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: iccDEV JSON config feature test suite — 90 tests, 0 ASAN

Test infrastructure for iccDEV -cfg JSON configuration feature used by
iccApplyNamedCmm, iccApplyProfiles, and iccApplySearch tools.

Test suite:
- 9 valid JSON configs (7 ApplyNamedCmm + 2 ApplySearch)
- 14 malformed JSON configs for negative/security testing
- Automated test runner (90 tests across 10 sections)
- 0 ASAN/UBSAN findings, 0 test failures

Findings documented in README.md:
- Bug: dstDigits]" typo in IccCmmConfig.cpp:303 (CWE-1288)
- Encoding strings must be "float"/"8Bit"/etc, not enum integers
- Empty profileSequence accepted gracefully (exit 0)
- Extreme float values processed as zeros (overflow)
- Channel count mismatch zero-fills missing channels

JSON schema reference with all valid encoding/type/intent values.

- Reduce chat page padding on mobile (px-2/pb-2/pt-1 vs px-4/pb-4/pt-2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: AuthenticatedHttpMessageHandler tolerates missing AzureAd:Scopes

In the WebApp, this handler is registered but unused (the WebApp has its
own OIDC-based AuthenticatedApiDelegatingHandler). Previously it threw
on construction if AzureAd:Scopes was missing. Now it defaults to empty
scopes and short-circuits SendAsync — a no-op passthrough.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: WebApp needs no-op IAuthService, not MsalAuthService

WebApp uses OIDC for auth, not MSAL. Registering AddAuthServices
instantiated MsalAuthService which requires AzureAd:Scopes config
meant for MAUI clients. Instead, register DevAuthService as a no-op
so AppLib's AuthenticatedHttpMessageHandler resolves in DI, while
the WebApp's own AuthenticatedApiDelegatingHandler handles real tokens.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: register AddAuthServices in WebApp before AddApiClients

AuthenticatedHttpMessageHandler requires IAuthService in DI.
WebApp called AddApiClients (which adds the handler) without first
calling AddAuthServices (which registers IAuthService + the handler).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve post-merge build errors across WebApp and UI projects

- WebApp: AddMicrosoftIdentityWebApp requires AddAuthentication() first
- WebApp: GetAccessTokenForUserAsync parameter name mismatch
- UI/Vocabulary: stub unimplemented DuplicateGroup, BulkUpdateLemmas features
- Shared: UpdateVocabularyWordTermsAsync takes string PK (not int)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: MsalAuthService.SignInAsync return type matches IAuthService (AuthResult)

The merge of PRs #71 and #72 left a mismatch — MsalAuthService returned
AuthenticationResult instead of the abstracted AuthResult record.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add usage.md, architecture.md; update project docs (#22)

Add end-user documentation and update existing docs to reflect Phase 3/4 implementation.

- usage.md: setup guide, Docker workflow, DB inspection, pattern analyzer CLI
- architecture.md: component diagram, data flow, DB schema, external interfaces
- spotify-ad-analyzer.md: mark Phase 3/4 as implemented
- .github/copilot-instructions.md: pyannote 4.x, new env vars (HF_TOKEN, DIARIZE_MODEL, OLLAMA_MODEL, WATCHDOG_FORCE_POLLING)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix dark mode text contrast in chat UI

- Lighten --muted-foreground from #a09990 to #b8b0a8 for better contrast
  on dark backgrounds (~7:1 ratio vs previous ~4.6:1)
- Override Tailwind Typography prose colors for dark mode so chat message
  text inherits proper colors instead of using light-mode defaults
- Prose body text now inherits from parent element, ensuring user messages
  (muted) and assistant messages (foreground) both have readable contrast

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(dicts): auto-merge 379 recommended entries from run 23099481233

Updated 8 dictionary files with LibFuzzer recommended entries.
Source: ClusterFuzzLite Campaign run 23099481233

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix alternatives feature and add dual model constants

  generateText + manual JSON parsing, since the Copilot API does not
  support Structured Outputs (same fix applied to generateRecipeFromLikes
  earlier)
- Fix generateRecipeTags with the same generateObject → generateText
  migration
- Add PRIMARY_MODEL (claude-sonnet-4.6) and FAST_MODEL (claude-haiku-4.5)
  constants
- Use FAST_MODEL for thread title generation and ingredient alternatives
- Remove unused generateObject import from recipes.ts
